task_completion.description: Log into the OpenSIS application as admin.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenSIS dashboard is visible, indicating a successful login with the provided admin credentials.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the course configuration page for 'Anatomy & Physiology' (BIO201).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen shows the course details or catalog entry specifically for BIO201 (may be titled 'Human Anatomy & Physiology I' or 'Anatomy & Physiology').

task_completion.description: Access the prerequisite or course requirements section for BIO201.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A tab, modal, or section specifically for managing 'Prerequisites', 'Requirements', or 'Course Reqs' is open within the BIO201 course settings.

task_completion.description: Select 'Biology I' (BIO101) as the prerequisite course.
task_completion.visual_evidence: BIO101 (or 'General Biology I' / 'Biology I') is selected in a dropdown, search result, or input field designated for adding a prerequisite.

task_completion.description: Save the configuration making BIO101 a mandatory prerequisite for BIO201.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message is displayed, or the saved course requirements list shows BIO101 as an active, mandatory prerequisite for BIO201.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the OpenSIS web interface rather than using command-line tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: The web browser is used to navigate the OpenSIS application. There are no terminal windows showing curl requests or direct API calls.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the UI by directly modifying the database.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows open showing MySQL/MariaDB prompts or SQL INSERT/UPDATE commands targeting the 'course_reqs' table.

integrity.description: The configuration was performed in the actual application, not mocked in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The prerequisite configuration is visible within the standard OpenSIS application layout, not typed into a notepad or text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target course 'Anatomy & Physiology' (BIO201) may be officially listed in the system as 'Human Anatomy & Physiology I'. The prerequisite course 'Biology I' (BIO101) may be officially listed as 'General Biology I'. The application being configured is OpenSIS.
